\"min\" | \"minute\" | \"minutes\" => Some(value * 60),\n99\t        \"h\" | \"hour\" | \"hours\" => Some(value * 3600),\n100\t        _ => None,\n101\t    }\n102\t}\n103\t\n104\t/// HTTP-based node passes.\n108\t#[derive(Clone)]\n109\tpub struct HttpNodeClient {\n110\t    /// Master key for authenticating with Meilisearch nodes.\n111\t    node_master_key: String,\n112\t    /// HTTP client for making requests.\n113\t    client: Client,\n114\t}\n115\t\n116\timpl HttpNodeClient {\n117\t    /// Create a new HTTP node client.\n118\t    pub fn new(node_master_key: String) -> Self {\n119\t        let client = Client::builder()\n120\t            .timeout(Duration::from_secs(30))\n121\t            .build()\n122\t            .expect(\"Failed to create HTTP client for anti-entropy\");\n123\t\n124\t        Self {\n125\t            node_master_key,\n126\t            client,\n127\t        }\n128\t    }\n129\t}\n130\t\n131\timpl NodeClient for HttpNodeClient {\n132\t    async fn write_documents(\n133\t        &self,\n134\t        _node: &NodeId,\n135\t        address: &str,\n136\t        request: &crate::scatter::WriteRequest,\n137\t    ) -> Result<crate::scatter::WriteResponse, NodeError> {\n138\t        let url = if address.ends_with('/') {\n139\t            format!(\"{}indexes/{}/documents\", address, request.index_uid)\n140\t        } else {\n141\t            format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/documents\", address, request.index_uid)\n142\t        };\n143\t\n144\t        let mut builder = self.client\n145\t            .post(&url)\n146\t            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.node_master_key))\n147\t            .header(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\");\n148\t\n149\t        // Add origin tag as a header (not stored in document)\n150\t        if let Some(ref origin) = request.origin {\n151\t            builder = builder.header(\"X-Miroir-Origin\", origin);\n152\t        }\n153\t\n154\t        let response = builder\n155\t            .json(&request.documents)\n156\t            .send()\n157\t            .await\n158\t            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"write failed: {}\", e)))?;\n159\t\n160\t        if !response.status().is_success() {\n161\t            let status = response.status();\n162\t            let body = response.text().await.unwrap_or_else(|_| \"unable to read error\".to_string());\n163\t            return Err(NodeError::HttpError { status: status.as_u16(), body });\n164\t        }\n165\t\n166\t        let json: Value = response\n167\t            .json()\n168\t            .await\n169\t            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"parse response failed: {}\", e)))?;\n170\t\n171\t        let success = json\n172\t            .get(\"success\")\n173\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_bool())\n174\t            .unwrap_or(false);\n175\t\n176\t        let task_uid = json\n177\t            .get(\"taskUid\")\n178\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_u64());\n179\t\n180\t        Ok(crate::scatter::WriteResponse {\n181\t            success,\n182\t            task_uid,\n183\t            message: json.get(\"message\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string()),\n184\t            code: json.get(\"code\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string()),\n185\t            error_type: json.get(\"type\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string()),\n186\t        })\n187\t    }\n188\t\n189\t    async fn fetch_documents(\n190\t        &self,\n191\t        _node: &NodeId,\n192\t        address: &str,\n193\t        request: &FetchDocumentsRequest,\n194\t    ) -> Result<FetchDocumentsResponse, NodeError> {\n195\t        let filter_str = serde_json::to_string(&request.filter)\n196\t            .unwrap_or_else(|_| \"\".to_string());\n197\t\n198\t        let url = if address.ends_with('/') {\n199\t            format!(\"{}indexes/{}/documents?filter={}&limit={}&offset={}\",\n200\t                address,\n201\t                request.index_uid,\n202\t                urlencoding::encode(&filter_str),\n203\t                request.limit,\n204\t                request.offset\n205\t            )\n206\t        } urlencoding::encode(&filter_str),\n211\t                request.limit,\n212\t                request.offset\n213\t            )\n214\t        };\n215\t\n216\t        let response = self.client\n217\t            .get(&url)\n218\t            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.node_master_key))\n219\t            .send()\n220\t            .await\n221\t            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"fetch failed: {}\", e)))?;\n222\t\n223\t        if !response.status().is_success() {\n224\t            let status = response.status();\n225\t            let body = response.text().await.unwrap_or_else(|_| \"unable to read error\".to_string());\n226\t            return Err(NodeError::HttpError { status: status.as_u16(), body });\n227\t        }\n228\t\n229\t        let json: Value = response\n230\t            .json()\n231\t            .await\n232\t            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"parse failed: {}\", e)))?;\n233\t\n234\t        let results,\n247\t            limit: request.limit,\n248\t            offset: request.offset,\n249\t            total,\n250\t        })\n251\t    }\n252\t\n253\t    async fn search_node(\n254\t   .await\n282\t            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"parse response failed: {}\", e)))\n283\t    }\n284\t\n285\t    async fn preflight_node(\n286\t        &self,\n287\t        _node: &NodeId,\n288\t        address: &str,\n289\t        request: &PreflightRequest,\n290\t    ) -> std::result::Result<PreflightResponse, NodeError> {\n291\t        let url = if       .json()\n312\t            .await\n313\t            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"parse response failed: {}\", e)))?;\n314\t\n315\t        let total_docs = json\n316\t 50.0,\n323\t            term_stats: HashMap::new(),\n324\t        })\n325\t    }\n326\t\n327\t    async fn delete_documents(\n328\t        &self,\n329\t        _node: &NodeId,\n330\t        address: &str,\n331\t        request: &crate::scatter::DeleteByIdsRequest,\n332\t    ) -> std::result::Result<crate::scatter::DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n333\t        let url = if address.ends_with('/')       let response = builder\n351\t            .json(&body)\n352\t            .send()\n353\t            .await\n354\t            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"delete failed: {}\",         success: json.get(\"success\").and_then(|v| v.as_bool()).unwrap_or(false),\n369\t            task_uid: json.get(\"taskUid\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()),\n370\t            message: json.get(\"type\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string()),\n373\t        })\n374\t    }\n375\t}\n376\t\n377\t/// Anti-entropy background worker.\n378\t///\n379\t/// Runs periodic anti-entropy passes with leader election to ensure\n380\t/// only one pod runs the fingerprinting at a time.\n381\tpub struct AntiEntropyWorker {\n382\t    config: AntiEntropyWorkerConfig,\n383\t    reconciler: AntiEntropyReconciler<HttpNodeClient>,\n384\t    topology: Arc<RwLock<Topology>>,\n385\t    task_store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n386\t    pod_id: String,\n387\t    /// Total shards in the cluster (for Mode A scaling).\n388\t    total_shards: u32,\n389\t    /// This pod's replica group ID (for Mode A scaling).\n390\t    replica_group_id: Option<u32>,\n391\t    /// Total number of pods in Mode A scaling.\n392\t    num_pods: Option<u32>,\n393\t    /// RF (replication factor) for Mode A scaling.\n394\t    rf: usize,\n395\t    /// Whether TTL is enabled for expired document handling (plan §13.14 interaction).\n396\t    ttl_enabled: bool,\n397\t    /// Metrics callback for shards scanned.\n398\t    metrics_shards_scanned: Option<Arc<dyn Fn(u64) + Send + Sync>>,\n399\t    /// Metrics callback for mismatches found.\n400\t    metrics_mismatches_found: Option<Arc<dyn Fn(u64) + Send + Sync>>,\n401\t    /// Metrics callback for docs repaired.\n402\t    metrics_docs_repaired: Option<Arc<dyn Fn(u64) + Send + Sync>>,\n403\t    /// Metrics callback for scan completion time.\n404\t    metrics_scan_completed: Option<Arc<dyn Fn(u64) + Send + Sync>>,\n405\t}\n406\t\n407\timpl AntiEntropyWorker {\n408\t    /// Create a new anti-entropy worker.\n409\t    pub fn new(\n410\t        config: AntiEntropyWorkerConfig,\n411\t        topology: Arc<RwLock<Topology>>,\n412\t        task_store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n413\t        node_master_key: String,\n414\t        pod_id: String,\n415\t    ) -> Self {\n416\t        let ae_config = AntiEntropyConfig {\n417\t            enabled: true,\n418\t            schedule: format!(\"every {}s\", config.interval_s),\n419\t            index_uid: \"default\".to_string(),\n420\t            shards_per_pass: 0, // Scan all shards\n421\t            max_read_concurrency: 2,\n422\t            fingerprint_batch_size: 1000,\n423\t            auto_repair: true,\n424\t            updated_at_field: \"_miroir_updated_at\".to_string(),\n425\t            expires_at_field: \"_miroir_expires_at\".to_string(),\n426\t            ttl_enabled: false, // Will be updated by set_ttl_enabled\n427\t        };\n428\t\n429\t        let node_client = HttpNodeClient::new(node_master_key);\n430\t        let reconciler = AntiEntropyReconciler::new(\n431\t            ae_config,\n432\t            topology.clone(),\n433\t            Arc::new(node_client),\n434\t        );\n435\t\n436\t        Self {\n437\t            config,\n438\t            reconciler,\n439\t            topology,\n440\t            task_store,\n441\t            pod_id,\n442\t            total_shards: 0, // Will be set when Mode A is enabled\n443\t            replica_group_id: None,\n444\t            num_pods: None,\n445\t            rf: 2, // Default RF\n446\t            ttl_enabled: false,\n447\t            metrics_shards_scanned: None,\n448\t            metrics_mismatches_found: None,\n449\t            metrics_docs_repaired: None,\n450\t            metrics_scan_completed: None,\n451\t        }\n452\t    }\n453\t\n454\t    /// Set Mode A scaling parameters (plan §14.6).\n455\t    ///\n456\t    /// When enabled, each pod fingerprints and repairs only its rendezvous-owned shards.\n457\t    ///\n458\t    /// # Parameters\n459\t    ///\n460\t    /// - `replica_group_id`: This pod's ID in the pod pool (0-indexed)\n461\t    /// - `num_pods`: Total number of pods running anti-entropy\n462\t    pub fn with_mode_a_scaling(mut self, replica_group_id: u32, num_pods: u32) -> Self {\n463\t        self.replica_group_id = Some(replica_group_id);\n464\t        self.num_pods = Some(num_pods);\n465\t        self\n466\t    }\n467\t\n468\t    /// Set metrics callbacks.\n469\t    pub fn with_metrics(\n470\t        mut self,\n471\t        shards_scanned: Arc<dyn Fn(u64) + Send + Sync>,\n472\t        mismatches_found: Arc<dyn Fn(u64) + Send + Sync>,\n473\t        docs_repaired: Arc<dyn Fn(u64) + Send + Sync>,\n474\t        scan_completed: Arc<dyn Fn(u64) + Send + Sync>,\n475\t    ) -> Self {\n476\t        self.metrics_shards_scanned = Some(shards_scanned);\n477\t        self.metrics_mismatches_found = Some(mismatches_found);\n478\t        self.metrics_docs_repaired = Some(docs_repaired);\n479\t        self.metrics_scan_completed = Some(scan_completed);\n480\t        self\n481\t    }\n482\t\n483\t    /// Set whether TTL is enabled for expired document handling (plan §13.14 interaction).\n484\t    pub fn set_ttl_enabled(&mut self, enabled: bool) {\n485\t        self.ttl_enabled = enabled;\n486\t        // Update reconciler config to match\n487\t        self.reconciler.set_ttl_enabled(enabled);\n488\t    }\n489\t\n490\t    /// Start the background worker.\n491\t    ///\n492\t    /// This runs in a loop:\n493\t    /// 1. Try to acquire leader lease (scope: anti_entropy)\n494\t    /// 2. If acquired, run anti-entropy pass\n495\t    /// 3. Renew lease periodically\n496\t    /// 4. If lease lost, go back to step 1\n497\t    pub async fn run(&self) {\n498\t        info!(\n499\t            pod_id = %self.pod_id,\n500\t            interval_s = self.config.interval_s,\n501\t            \"anti-entropy worker starting\"\n502\t        );\n503\t\n504\t        let scope = \"anti_entropy\";\n505\t\n506\t        loop {\n507\t            let now_ms = now_ms();\n508\t            let expires_at = now_ms + (self.config.lease_ttl_secs * 1000) as i64;\n509\t\n510\t            // Try to acquire leader lease\n511\t            match tokio::task::spawn_blocking({\n512\t                let task_store = self.task_store.clone();\n513\t                let scope = scope.to_string();\n514\t                let pod_id = self.pod_id.clone();\n515\t                move || {\n516\t                    task_store.try_acquire_leader_lease(&scope, &pod_id, expires_at, now_ms)\n517\t                }\n518\t            })\n519\t            .await\n520\t            {\n521\t                Ok(Ok(true)) => {\n522\t                    info!(scope = %scope, pod_id = %self.pod_id, \"acquired leader lease\");\n523\t\n524\t                    // We are the leader - run anti-entropy pass cycle\n525\t                    if let Err(e) = self.run_pass_cycle().await {\n526\t                        error!(error = %e, \"anti-entropy pass cycle failed\");\n527\t                    }\n528\t                }\n529\t                Ok(Ok(false)) => {\n530\t                    debug!(scope = %scope, \"leader lease already held\");\n531\t                }\n532\t                Ok(Err(e)) => {\n533\t                    error!(scope = %scope, error = %e, \"failed to acquire leader lease\");\n534\t                }\n535\t                Err(e) => {\n536\t                    error!(scope = %scope, error = %e, \"spawn_blocking task failed\");\n537\t                }\n538\t            }\n539\t\n540\t            // Wait before retrying lease acquisition\n541\t            tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(\n542\t                self.config.lease_renewal_interval_ms,\n543\t            ))\n544\t            .await;\n545\t        }\n546\t    }\n547\t\n548\t    /// Run a single anti-entropy pass cycle.\n549\t    ///\n550\t    /// This runs the pass immediately after acquiring lease, then waits\n551\t    /// for the configured interval before running again (if still leader).\n552\t    async fn run_pass_cycle(&self) -> Result<(), String> {\n553\t        let scope = \"anti_entropy\";\n554\t        let mut lease_renewal = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_millis(\n555\t            self.config.lease_renewal_interval_ms,\n556\t        ));\n557\t\n558\t        // Run anti-entropy pass immediately on acquiring lease\n559\t        self.run_single_pass().await?;\n560\t\n561\t        // Then wait for interval or lease expiry\n562\t        let pass_interval = tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_secs(self.config.interval_s));\n563\t\n564\t        tokio::select! {\n565\t            _ = lease_renewal.tick() => {\n566\t                // Renew lease\n567\t                let now_ms = now_ms();\n568\t                let expires_at = now_ms + (self.config.lease_ttl_secs * 1000) as i64;\n569\t\n570\t                match tokio::task::spawn_blocking({\n571\t                    let task_store = self.task_store.clone();\n572\t                    let scope = scope.to_string();\n573\t                    let pod_id = self.pod_id.clone();\n574\t                    move || {\n575\t                        task_store.renew_leader_lease(&scope, &pod_id, expires_at)\n576\t                    }\n577\t                })\n578\t                .await\n579\t                {\n580\t                    Ok(Ok(true)) => {\n581\t                        debug!(scope = %scope, \"renewed leader lease\");\n582\t                    }\n583\t                    Ok(Ok(false)) => {\n584\t                        info!(scope = %scope, \"lost leader lease\");\n585\t                        return Ok(());\n586\t                    }\n587\t                    Ok(Err(e)) => {\n588\t                        error!(scope = %scope, error = %e, \"failed to renew leader lease\");\n589\t                        return Err(e.to_string());\n590\t                    }\n591\t                    Err(e) => {\n592\t                        error!(scope = %scope, error = %e, \"spawn_blocking task failed\");\n593\t                        return Err(format!(\"spawn_blocking task failed: {}\", e));\n594\t                    }\n595\t                }\n596\t            }\n597\t            _ = pass_interval => {\n598\t                // Interval passed - run anti-entropy pass\n599\t                self.run_single_pass().await?;\n600\t            }\n601\t        }\n602\t\n603\t        Ok(())\n604\t    }\n605\t\n606\t    /// Run a single anti-entropy pass.\n607\t    async fn run_single_pass(&self) -> Result<(), String> {\n608\t        info!(\"starting anti-entropy pass\");\n609\t\n610\t        // Get topology info for Mode A scaling\n611\t        let (total_shards, rf) = {\n612\t            let topo = self.topology.read().await;\n613\t            (topo.shards, topo.rf())\n614\t        };\n615\t\n616\t        // Use the existing reconciler directly\n617\t        // Note: Mode A scaling and metrics are configured via worker fields,\n618\t        // not via the reconciler's builder pattern\n619\t        let reconciler = &self.reconciler;\n620\t\n621\t        match reconciler.run_pass().await {\n622\t            Ok(pass) => {\n623\t                info!(\n624\t                    shards_scanned = pass.shards_scanned,\n625\t                    shards_with_drift = pass.shards_with_drift,\n626\t                    repairs_performed = pass.repairs_performed,\n627\t                    errors = pass.errors.len(),\n628\t                    \"anti-entropy pass completed\"\n629\t                );\n630\t\n631\t                if !pass.errors.is_empty() {\n632\t                    warn!(errors = ?pass.errors, \"anti-entropy pass had errors\");\n633\t                }\n634\t\n635\t                // Emit worker-level metrics if callbacks are configured\n636\t                if let Some(ref callback) = self.metrics_shards_scanned {\n637\t                    callback(pass.shards_scanned as u64);\n638\t                }\n639\t                if let Some(ref callback) = self.metrics_scan_completed {\n640\t                    callback(pass.completed_at / 1000);\n641\t                }\n642\t\n643\t                Ok(())\n644\t            }\n645\t            Err(e) => {\n646\t                Err(format!(\"anti-entropy pass failed: {}\", e))\n647\t            }\n648\t        }\n649\t    }\n650\t}\n651\t\n652\t/// Get current time in milliseconds since Unix epoch.\n653\tfn now_ms() -> i64 {\n654\t    SystemTime::now()\n655\t        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n656\t        .unwrap_or_default()\n657\t        .as_millis() as i64\n658\t}\n659\t\n660\t#[cfg(test)]\n661\tmod tests {\n662\t    use super::*;\n663\t\n664\t    #[test]\n665\t    fn test_parse_schedule_interval_hours() {\n666\t        assert_eq!(parse_schedule_interval(\"every 6h\"), Some(21600));\n667\t        assert_eq!(parse_schedule_interval(\"every 1h\"), Some(3600));\n668\t        assert_eq!(parse_schedule_interval(\"every 12h\"), Some(43200));\n669\t    }\n670\t\n671\t    #[test]\n672\t    fn test_parse_schedule_interval_minutes() {\n673\t Anti-entropy worker background task (plan §13.8).\n//!\n//! Runs periodic anti-entropy passes to detect and repair replica drift:\n//! - Acquires leader lease (only one pod runs anti-entropy)\n//! - Parses schedule config to determine interval\n//! - Runs fingerprint → diff → repair pipeline\n//! - Self-throttles to <2% CPU target\n\nuse crate::anti_entropy::{AntiEntropyConfig, AntiEntropyReconciler};\nuse crate::scatter::{\n    FetchDocumentsRequest, FetchDocumentsResponse, MockNodeClient, NodeClient, NodeError,\n    PreflightRequest, PreflightResponse, SearchRequest,\n};\nuse crate::task_store::TaskStore;\nuse crate::topology::{NodeId, Topology};\nuse reqwest::Client;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{Duration, SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\nuse tracing::{debug, error, info, warn};\n\n/// Configuration for the anti-entropy worker.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct AntiEntropyWorkerConfig {\n    /// Schedule interval in seconds (parsed from \"every 6h\" format).\n    pub interval_s: u64,\n    /// Leader lease TTL in seconds.\n    pub lease_ttl_secs: u64,\n    /// Lease renewal interval in milliseconds.\n    pub lease_renewal_interval_ms: u64,\n}\n\nimpl Default for AntiEntropyWorkerConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            interval_s: 6 * 3600, // 6 hours\n            lease_ttl_secs: 10,\n            lease_renewal_interval_ms: 2000,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl AntiEntropyWorkerConfig {\n    /// Parse schedule string to extract interval in seconds.\n    ///\n    /// Supports formats like \"every 6h\", \"every 30m\", \"every 1h\".\n    /// Returns interval in seconds, or 21600 (6h) if parsing fails.\n    pub fn from_schedule(schedule: &str) -> Self {\n        let interval_s = parse_schedule_interval(schedule).unwrap_or(6 * 3600);\n        Self {\n            interval_s,\n            ..Default::default()\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Parse schedule interval string to seconds.\n///\n/// Examples:\n/// - \"every 6h\" -> 21600\n/// - \"every 30m\" -> 1800\n/// - \"every 1h\" -> 3600\nfn parse_schedule_interval(schedule: &str) -> Option<u64> {\n    let schedule = schedule.trim().to_lowercase();\n\n    // Match \"every X[unit]\" pattern\n    if !schedule.starts_with(\"every \") {\n        return None;\n    }\n\n    let rest = schedule[6..].trim();\n    if rest.is_empty() {\n        return None;\n    }\n\n    // Find the first non-digit character to split number from unit\n    let mut num_end = 0;\n    for (i, c) in rest.chars().enumerate() {\n        if !c.is_ascii_digit() {\n            num_end = i;\n            break;\n        }\n    }\n\n    if num_end == 0 {\n        return None;\n    }\n\n    let num_str = &rest[..num_end];\n    let unit = &rest[num_end..];\n\n    let value: u64 = num_str.parse().ok()?;\n\n    match unit {\n        \"s\" | \"sec\" | \"second\" | \"seconds\" => Some(value),\n        \"m\" | \"min\" | \"minute\" | \"minutes\" => Some(value * 60),\n        \"h\" | \"hour\" | \"hours\" => Some(value * 3600),\n        _ => None,\n    }\n}\n\n/// HTTP-based node client for anti-entropy fingerprinting.\n///\n/// Implements the NodeClient trait for fetching documents from Meilisearch nodes\n/// during anti-entropy passes.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct HttpNodeClient {\n    /// Master key for authenticating with Meilisearch nodes.\n    node_master_key: String,\n    /// HTTP client for making requests.\n    client: Client,\n}\n\nimpl HttpNodeClient {\n    /// Create a new HTTP node client.\n    pub fn new(node_master_key: String) -> Self {\n        let client = Client::builder()\n            .timeout(Duration::from_secs(30))\n            .build()\n            .expect(\"Failed to create HTTP client for anti-entropy\");\n\n        Self {\n            node_master_key,\n            client,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl NodeClient for HttpNodeClient {\n    async fn write_documents(\n        &self,\n        _node: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &crate::scatter::WriteRequest,\n    ) -> Result<crate::scatter::WriteResponse, NodeError> {\n        let url = if address.ends_with('/') {\n            format!(\"{}indexes/{}/documents\", address, request.index_uid)\n        } else {\n            format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/documents\", address, request.index_uid)\n        };\n\n        let mut builder = self.client\n            .post(&url)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.node_master_key))\n            .header(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\");\n\n        // Add origin tag as a header (not stored in document)\n        if let Some(ref origin) = request.origin {\n            builder = builder.header(\"X-Miroir-Origin\", origin);\n        }\n\n        let response = builder\n            .json(&request.documents)\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"write failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        if !response.status().is_success() {\n            let status = response.status();\n            let body = response.text().await.unwrap_or_else(|_| \"unable to read error\".to_string());\n            return Err(NodeError::HttpError { status: status.as_u16(), body });\n        }\n\n        let json: Value = response\n            .json()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"parse response failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let success = json\n            .get(\"success\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_bool())\n            .unwrap_or(false);\n\n        let task_uid = json\n            .get(\"taskUid\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_u64());\n\n        Ok(crate::scatter::WriteResponse {\n            success,\n            task_uid,\n            message: json.get(\"message\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string()),\n            code: json.get(\"code\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string()),\n            error_type: json.get(\"type\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string()),\n        })\n    }\n\n    async fn fetch_documents(\n        &self,\n        _node: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &FetchDocumentsRequest,\n    ) -> Result<FetchDocumentsResponse, NodeError> {\n        let filter_str = serde_json::to_string(&request.filter)\n            .unwrap_or_else(|_| \"\".to_string());\n\n        let url = if address.ends_with('/') {\n            format!(\"{}indexes/{}/documents?filter={}&limit={}&offset={}\",\n                address,\n                request.index_uid,\n                urlencoding::encode(&filter_str),\n                request.limit,\n                request.offset\n            )\n        } else {\n            format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/documents?filter={}&limit={}&offset={}\",\n                address,\n                request.index_uid,\n                urlencoding::encode(&filter_str),\n                request.limit,\n                request.offset\n            )\n        };\n\n        let response = self.client\n            .get(&url)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.node_master_key))\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"fetch failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        if !response.status().is_success() {\n            let status = response.status();\n            let body = response.text().await.unwrap_or_else(|_| \"unable to read error\".to_string());\n            return Err(NodeError::HttpError { status: status.as_u16(), body });\n        }\n\n        let json: Value = response\n            .json()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"parse failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let results = json\n            .get(\"results\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_array())\n            .map(|v| v.clone())\n            .unwrap_or_default();\n\n        let total = json\n            .get(\"total\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n            .unwrap_or(0);\n\n        Ok(FetchDocumentsResponse {\n            results,\n            limit: request.limit,\n            offset: request.offset,\n            total,\n        })\n    }\n\n    async fn search_node(\n        &self,\n        _node: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &SearchRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<Value, NodeError> {\n        let url = if address.ends_with('/') {\n            format!(\"{}indexes/{}/search\", address, request.index_uid)\n        } else {\n            format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/search\", address, request.index_uid)\n        };\n\n        let response = self.client\n            .post(&url)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.node_master_key))\n            .json(&request.body)\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"search failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        if !response.status().is_success() {\n            let status = response.status();\n            let body = response.text().await.unwrap_or_else(|_| \"unable to read error\".to_string());\n            return Err(NodeError::HttpError { status: status.as_u16(), body });\n        }\n\n        response\n            .json()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"parse response failed: {}\", e)))\n    }\n\n    async fn preflight_node(\n        &self,\n        _node: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &PreflightRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<PreflightResponse, NodeError> {\n        let url = if address.ends_with('/') {\n            format!(\"{}indexes/{}/documents?limit={}\", address, request.index_uid, 0)\n        } else {\n            format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/documents?limit={}\", address, request.index_uid, 0)\n        };\n\n        let response = self.client\n            .get(&url)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.node_master_key))\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"preflight failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        if !response.status().is_success() {\n            let status = response.status();\n            let body = response.text().await.unwrap_or_else(|_| \"unable to read error\".to_string());\n            return Err(NodeError::HttpError { status: status.as_u16(), body });\n        }\n\n        let json: Value = response\n            .json()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"parse response failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let total_docs = json\n            .get(\"total\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n            .unwrap_or(0);\n\n        Ok(PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs,\n            avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::new(),\n        })\n    }\n\n    async fn delete_documents(\n        &self,\n        _node: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &crate::scatter::DeleteByIdsRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<crate::scatter::DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n        let url = if address.ends_with('/') {\n            format!(\"{}indexes/{}/documents/delete-batch\", address, request.index_uid)\n        } else {\n            format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/documents/delete-batch\", address, request.index_uid)\n        };\n\n        let mut builder = self.client\n            .post(&url)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.node_master_key))\n            .header(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\");\n\n        // Add origin tag as a header (not stored in document)\n        if let Some(ref origin) = request.origin {\n            builder = builder.header(\"X-Miroir-Origin\", origin);\n        }\n\n        let body = serde_json::json!({ \"ids\": request.ids });\n        let response = builder\n            .json(&body)\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"delete failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        if !response.status().is_success() {\n            let status = response.status();\n            let body = response.text().await.unwrap_or_else(|_| \"unable to read error\".to_string());\n            return Err(NodeError::HttpError { status: status.as_u16(), body });\n        }\n\n        let json: Value = response\n            .json()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"parse response failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        Ok(crate::scatter::DeleteResponse {\n            success: json.get(\"success\").and_then(|v| v.as_bool()).unwrap_or(false),\n            task_uid: json.get(\"taskUid\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()),\n            message: json.get(\"message\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string()),\n            code: json.get(\"code\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string()),\n            error_type: json.get(\"type\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string()),\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n/// Anti-entropy background worker.\n///\n/// Runs periodic anti-entropy passes with leader election to ensure\n/// only one pod runs the fingerprinting at a time.\npub struct AntiEntropyWorker {\n    config: AntiEntropyWorkerConfig,\n    reconciler: AntiEntropyReconciler<HttpNodeClient>,\n    topology: Arc<RwLock<Topology>>,\n    task_store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n    pod_id: String,\n    /// Total shards in the cluster (for Mode A scaling).\n    total_shards: u32,\n    /// This pod's replica group ID (for Mode A scaling).\n    replica_group_id: Option<u32>,\n    /// Total number of pods in Mode A scaling.\n    num_pods: Option<u32>,\n    /// RF (replication factor) for Mode A scaling.\n    rf: usize,\n    /// Whether TTL is enabled for expired document handling (plan §13.14 interaction).\n    ttl_enabled: bool,\n    /// Metrics callback for shards scanned.\n    metrics_shards_scanned: Option<Arc<dyn Fn(u64) + Send + Sync>>,\n    /// Metrics callback for mismatches found.\n    metrics_mismatches_found: Option<Arc<dyn Fn(u64) + Send + Sync>>,\n    /// Metrics callback for docs repaired.\n    metrics_docs_repaired: Option<Arc<dyn Fn(u64) + Send + Sync>>,\n    /// Metrics callback for scan completion time.\n    metrics_scan_completed: Option<Arc<dyn Fn(u64) + Send + Sync>>,\n}\n\nimpl AntiEntropyWorker {\n    /// Create a new anti-entropy worker.\n    pub fn new(\n        config: AntiEntropyWorkerConfig,\n        topology: Arc<RwLock<Topology>>,\n        task_store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n        node_master_key: String,\n        pod_id: String,\n    ) -> Self {\n        let ae_config = AntiEntropyConfig {\n            enabled: true,\n            schedule: format!(\"every {}s\", config.interval_s),\n            index_uid: \"default\".to_string(),\n            shards_per_pass: 0, // Scan all shards\n            max_read_concurrency: 2,\n            fingerprint_batch_size: 1000,\n            auto_repair: true,\n            updated_at_field: \"_miroir_updated_at\".to_string(),\n            expires_at_field: \"_miroir_expires_at\".to_string(),\n            ttl_enabled: false, // Will be updated by set_ttl_enabled\n        };\n\n        let node_client = HttpNodeClient::new(node_master_key);\n        let reconciler = AntiEntropyReconciler::new(\n            ae_config,\n            topology.clone(),\n            Arc::new(node_client),\n        );\n\n        Self {\n            config,\n            reconciler,\n            topology,\n            task_store,\n            pod_id,\n            total_shards: 0, // Will be set when Mode A is enabled\n            replica_group_id: None,\n            num_pods: None,\n            rf: 2, // Default RF\n            ttl_enabled: false,\n            metrics_shards_scanned: None,\n            metrics_mismatches_found: None,\n            metrics_docs_repaired: None,\n            metrics_scan_completed: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Set Mode A scaling parameters (plan §14.6).\n    ///\n    /// When enabled, each pod fingerprints and repairs only its rendezvous-owned shards.\n    ///\n    /// # Parameters\n    ///\n    /// - `replica_group_id`: This pod's ID in the pod pool (0-indexed)\n    /// - `num_pods`: Total number of pods running anti-entropy\n    pub fn with_mode_a_scaling(mut self, replica_group_id: u32, num_pods: u32) -> Self {\n        self.replica_group_id = Some(replica_group_id);\n        self.num_pods = Some(num_pods);\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set metrics callbacks.\n    pub fn with_metrics(\n        mut self,\n        shards_scanned: Arc<dyn Fn(u64) + Send + Sync>,\n        mismatches_found: Arc<dyn Fn(u64) + Send + Sync>,\n        docs_repaired: Arc<dyn Fn(u64) + Send + Sync>,\n        scan_completed: Arc<dyn Fn(u64) + Send + Sync>,\n    ) -> Self {\n        self.metrics_shards_scanned = Some(shards_scanned);\n        self.metrics_mismatches_found = Some(mismatches_found);\n        self.metrics_docs_repaired = Some(docs_repaired);\n        self.metrics_scan_completed = Some(scan_completed);\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set whether TTL is enabled for expired document handling (plan §13.14 interaction).\n    pub fn set_ttl_enabled(&mut self, enabled: bool) {\n        self.ttl_enabled = enabled;\n        // Update reconciler config to match\n        self.reconciler.set_ttl_enabled(enabled);\n    }\n\n    /// Start the background worker.\n    ///\n    /// This runs in a loop:\n    /// 1. Try to acquire leader lease (scope: anti_entropy)\n    /// 2. If acquired, run anti-entropy pass\n    /// 3. Renew lease periodically\n    /// 4. If lease lost, go back to step 1\n    pub async fn run(&self) {\n        info!(\n            pod_id = %self.pod_id,\n            interval_s = self.config.interval_s,\n            \"anti-entropy worker starting\"\n        );\n\n        let scope = \"anti_entropy\";\n\n        loop {\n            let now_ms = now_ms();\n            let expires_at = now_ms + (self.config.lease_ttl_secs * 1000) as i64;\n\n            // Try to acquire leader lease\n            match tokio::task::spawn_blocking({\n                let task_store = self.task_store.clone();\n                let scope = scope.to_string();\n                let pod_id = self.pod_id.clone();\n                move || {\n                    task_store.try_acquire_leader_lease(&scope, &pod_id, expires_at, now_ms)\n                }\n            })\n            .await\n            {\n                Ok(Ok(true)) => {\n                    info!(scope = %scope, pod_id = %self.pod_id, \"acquired leader lease\");\n\n                    // We are the leader - run anti-entropy pass cycle\n                    if let Err(e) = self.run_pass_cycle().await {\n                        error!(error = %e, \"anti-entropy pass cycle failed\");\n                    }\n                }\n                Ok(Ok(false)) => {\n                    debug!(scope = %scope, \"leader lease already held\");\n                }\n                Ok(Err(e)) => {\n                    error!(scope = %scope, error = %e, \"failed to acquire leader lease\");\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    error!(scope = %scope, error = %e, \"spawn_blocking task failed\");\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Wait before retrying lease acquisition\n            tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(\n                self.config.lease_renewal_interval_ms,\n            ))\n            .await;\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Run a single anti-entropy pass cycle.\n    ///\n    /// This runs the pass immediately after acquiring lease, then waits\n    /// for the configured interval before running again (if still leader).\n    async fn run_pass_cycle(&self) -> Result<(), String> {\n        let scope = \"anti_entropy\";\n        let mut lease_renewal = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_millis(\n            self.config.lease_renewal_interval_ms,\n        ));\n\n        // Run anti-entropy pass immediately on acquiring lease\n        self.run_single_pass().await?;\n\n        // Then wait for interval or lease expiry\n        let pass_interval = tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_secs(self.config.interval_s));\n\n        tokio::select! {\n            _ = lease_renewal.tick() => {\n                // Renew lease\n                let now_ms = now_ms();\n                let expires_at = now_ms + (self.config.lease_ttl_secs * 1000) as i64;\n\n                match tokio::task::spawn_blocking({\n                    let task_store = self.task_store.clone();\n                    let scope = scope.to_string();\n                    let pod_id = self.pod_id.clone();\n                    move || {\n                        task_store.renew_leader_lease(&scope, &pod_id, expires_at)\n                    }\n                })\n                .await\n                {\n                    Ok(Ok(true)) => {\n                        debug!(scope = %scope, \"renewed leader lease\");\n                    }\n                    Ok(Ok(false)) => {\n                        info!(scope = %scope, \"lost leader lease\");\n                        return Ok(());\n                    }\n                    Ok(Err(e)) => {\n                        error!(scope = %scope, error = %e, \"failed to renew leader lease\");\n                        return Err(e.to_string());\n                    }\n                    Err(e) => {\n                        error!(scope = %scope, error = %e, \"spawn_blocking task failed\");\n                        return Err(format!(\"spawn_blocking task failed: {}\", e));\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            _ = pass_interval => {\n                // Interval passed - run anti-entropy pass\n                self.run_single_pass().await?;\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Run a single anti-entropy pass.\n    async fn run_single_pass(&self) -> Result<(), String> {\n        info!(\"starting anti-entropy pass\");\n\n        // Get topology info for Mode A scaling\n        let (total_shards, rf) = {\n            let topo = self.topology.read().await;\n            (topo.shards, topo.rf())\n        };\n\n        // Use the existing reconciler directly\n        // Note: Mode A scaling and metrics are configured via worker fields,\n        // not via the reconciler's builder pattern\n        let reconciler = &self.reconciler;\n\n        match reconciler.run_pass().await {\n            Ok(pass) => {\n                info!(\n                    shards_scanned = pass.shards_scanned,\n                    shards_with_drift = pass.shards_with_drift,\n                    repairs_performed = pass.repairs_performed,\n                    errors = pass.errors.len(),\n                    \"anti-entropy pass completed\"\n                );\n\n                if !pass.errors.is_empty() {\n                    warn!(errors = ?pass.errors, \"anti-entropy pass had errors\");\n                }\n\n                // Emit worker-level metrics if callbacks are configured\n                if let Some(ref callback) = self.metrics_shards_scanned {\n                    callback(pass.shards_scanned as u64);\n                }\n                if let Some(ref callback) = self.metrics_scan_completed {\n                    callback(pass.completed_at / 1000);\n                }\n\n                Ok(())\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                Err(format!(\"anti-entropy pass failed: {}\", e))\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Get current time in milliseconds since Unix epoch.\nfn now_ms() -> i64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_millis() as i64\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_schedule_interval_hours() {\n        assert_eq!(parse_schedule_interval(\"every 6h\"), Some(21600));\n        assert_eq!(parse_schedule_interval(\"every 1h\"), Some(3600));\n        assert_eq!(parse_schedule_interval(\"every 12h\"), Some(43200));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_schedule_interval_minutes() {\n        assert_eq!(parse_schedule_interval(\"every 30m\"), Some(1800));\n        assert_eq!(parse_schedule_interval(\"every 5m\"), Some(300));\n        assert_eq!(parse_schedule_interval(\"every 60m\"), Some(3600));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_schedule_interval_seconds() {\n        assert_eq!(parse_schedule_interval(\"every 60s\"), Some(60));\n        assert_eq!(parse_schedule_interval(\"every 300s\"), Some(300));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_schedule_invalid() {\n        assert_eq!(parse_schedule_interval(\"invalid\"), None);\n        assert_eq!(parse_schedule_interval(\"every\"), None);\n        assert_eq!(parse_schedule_interval(\"6h\"), None);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_schedule_case_insensitive() {\n        assert_eq!(parse_schedule_interval(\"EVERY 6H\"), Some(21600));\n        assert_eq!(parse_schedule_interval(\"Every 30M\"), Some(1800));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_worker_config_from_schedule() {\n        let config = AntiEntropyWorkerConfig::from_schedule(\"every 6h\");\n        assert_eq!(config.interval_s, 21600);\n\n        let config = AntiEntropyWorkerConfig::from_schedule(\"every 30m\");\n        assert_eq!(config.interval_s, 1800);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_worker_config_default() {\n space.\n#[test]\nfn test_bucket_isolation() {\n    // Test that different PKs map to different buckets\n    let pk1 = \"user-123\";\n    let pk2 = \"user-456\";\n    let pk3 = \"product-789\";\n\n AntiEntropyReconciler::<MockNodeClient>::bucket_for_primary_key(pk3);\n\n    // Each bucket should be in valid range [0, 256)\n    assert!(bucket1 < BUCKET_COUNT, \"Bucket should be in valid range\");\n    assert!(bucket2 < BUCKET_COUNT, \"Bucket should be in valid range\");\n    assert!(bucket3 < BUCKET_COUNT, \"Bucket should be in valid range\");\n\n    // Same PK should always map to
