hasher.write(primary_key.as_bytes());\n235\t        (hasher.finish() as usize) % BUCKET_COUNT\n236\t    }\n237\t\n238\t    /// Compute the canonical content hash of a document.\n239\t    ///\n240\t    /// The canonical form excludes internal Miroir fields (_miroir_*, _rankingScore)\n241\t    /// and serializes with sorted keys for deterministic hashing.\n242\t    pub fn compute_content_hash(document: &Value) -> u64 {\n243\t        // Remove internal fields to get canonical content\n244\t        let mut canonical = document.clone();\n245\t        if let Some(obj) = canonical.as_object_mut() {\n246\t            // Remove all _miroir_* fields\n247\t            obj.retain(|k, _| !k.starts_with(\"_miroir_\"));\n248\t            // Remove _rankingScore (not content, used for scoring)\n249\t            obj.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n250\t        }\n251\t\n252\t        // Serialize with sorted keys for deterministic output\n253\t        let canonical_json = if let Some(obj) = canonical.as_object() {\n254\t            // Use BTreeMap to sort keys\n255\t            let sorted: BTreeMap<_, _> = obj.iter().collect();\n256\t            serde_json::to_string(&sorted).unwrap_or_else(|_| \"{}\".to_string())\n257\t        } else {\n258\t            serde_json::to_string(&canonical).unwrap_or_else(|_| \"{}\".to_string())\n259\t        };\n260\t\n261\t        // Hash using xxh3 (xxhash family, same as router)\n262\t        let mut hasher = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n263\t        hasher.write(canonical_json.as_bytes());\n264\t        hasher.finish()\n265\t    }\n266\t\n267\t    /// Check if a document is expired (plan §13.14 interaction).\n268\t    ///\n269\t    /// Returns true if the document has an expires_at field that is in the past.\n270\t    /// Expired documents are treated as logically deleted by anti-entropy\n271\t    /// to prevent zombie resurrection.\n272\t    fn is_document_expired(document: &Value) -> bool {\n273\t        if let Some(expires_at) = document.get(\"_miroir_expires_at\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()) {\n274\t            let now_ms = SystemTime::now()\n275\t                .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n276\t                .unwrap_or_default()\n277\t                .as_millis() as u64;\n278\t            expires_at <= now_ms\n279\t        } else {\n280\t            false\n281\t        }\n282\t    }\n283\t\n284\t    /// Fingerprint a single shard on a node (plan §13.8 step 1).\n285\t    ///\n286\t    /// Iterates all documents with filter=_miroir_shard={id}, computes\n287\t    /// hash(primary_key || content_hash) for each, and folds into a\n288\t    /// streaming xxh3 digest. Also computes per-bucket hashes for diff.\n289\t    pub async fn fingerprint_shard(\n290\t        &self,\n291\t        node_id: &NodeId,\n292\t        shard_id: u32,\n293\t        index_uid: &str,\n294\t        address: &str,\n295\t    ) -> Result<ShardFingerprint> {\n296\t        let batch_size = self.config.fingerprint_batch_size as usize;\n297\t        let mut offset = 0u32;\n298\t        let mut document_count = 0u64;\n299\t        let mut hasher = XxHash64::with_seed(shard_id as u64); // Shard-seeded digest\n300\t\n301\t        // Per-bucket hashers for granular diff (plan §13.8 step 2)\n302\t        let mut bucket_hashers: Vec<XxHash64> = (0..BUCKET_COUNT)\n303\t            .map(|_| XxHash64::with_seed(shard_id as u64))\n304\t            .collect();\n305\t\n306\t        // Paginated iteration through documents\n307\t        loop {\n308\t            let filter = serde_json::json!({ \"_miroir_shard\": shard_id });\n309\t            let request = FetchDocumentsRequest {\n310\t                index_uid: index_uid.to_string(),\n311\t                filter,\n312\t                limit: batch_size as u32,\n313\t                offset,\n314\t            };\n315\t\n316\t            let response: FetchDocumentsResponse = self\n317\t                .node_client\n318\t                .fetch_documents(node_id, address, &request)\n319\t                .await\n320\t                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Topology(format!(\"fetch failed: {:?}\", e)))?;\n321\t\n322\t            if response.results.is_empty() {\n323\t                break; // No more documents\n324\t            }\n325\t\n326\t            for doc in &response.results {\n327\t                // Skip expired documents (plan §13.14 interaction)\n328\t                // Treats them as logically deleted to prevent zombie resurrection\n329\t                if self.config.ttl_enabled && Self::is_document_expired(doc) {\n330\t                    debug!(\n331\t                        shard_id,\n332\t                        primary_key = doc.get(\"id\").or(doc.get(\"_id\")).and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or(\"\"),\n333\t                        \"Skipping expired document in anti-entropy fingerprint\"\n334\t                    );\n335\t                    continue;\n336\t                }\n337\t\n338\t                // Extract primary key\n339\t                let primary_key = doc\n340\t                    .get(\"id\")\n341\t                    .or(doc.get(\"_id\"))\n342\t                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n343\t                    .unwrap_or(\"\");\n344\t\n345\t                // Compute content hash\n346\t                let content_hash = Self::compute_content_hash(doc);\n347\t\n348\t                // Fold: hash(primary_key || content_hash) into digest\n349\t                let mut pk_hasher = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n350\t                pk_hasher.write(primary_key.as_bytes());\n351\t                pk_hasher.write_u64(content_hash);\n352\t                let doc_hash = pk_hasher.finish();\n353\t\n354\t                // Fold into shard digest\n355\t                hasher.write_u64(doc_hash);\n356\t                document_count += 1;\n357\t\n358\t                // Fold into bucket digest for granular diff\n359\t                let bucket_id = Self::bucket_for_primary_key(primary_key);\n360\t                bucket_hashers[bucket_id].write_u64(doc_hash);\n361\t            }\n362\t\n363\t            offset += batch_size as u32;\n364\t\n365\t            // Self-throttle: small sleep between batches to target <2% CPU\n366\t            // (In production, this would be adaptive based on CPU metrics)\n367\t            tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_millis(10)).await;\n368\t        }\n369\t\n370\t        let merkle_root = format!(\"xxh3:{}\", hasher.finish());\n371\t\n372\t        // Extract per-bucket hashes\n373\t        let bucket_hashes: Vec<String> = bucket_hashers\n374\t            .into_iter()\n375\t            .map(|h| format!(\"xxh3:{}\", h.finish()))\n376\t            .collect();\n377\t\n378\t        debug!(\n379\t            \"Fingerprinted shard {} on node {}: {} documents, root {}\",\n380\t            shard_id, node_id, document_count, merkle_root\n381\t        );\n382\t\n383\t        Ok(ShardFingerprint {\n384\t            shard_id,\n385\t            node_id: node_id.to_string(),\n386\t            merkle_root,\n387\t            document_count,\n388\t            bucket_hashes,\n389\t        })\n390\t    }\n391\t\n392\t    /// Find divergent buckets between two fingerprints (plan §13.8 step 2).\n393\t    ///\n394\t    /// Returns bucket IDs where the per-bucket hashes differ.\n395\t    /// Each bucket isolates divergence to ~0.4% of the PK space.\n396\t    pub fn diff_fingerprints(\n397\t        &self,\n398\t        fp_a: &ShardFingerprint,\n399\t        fp_b: &ShardFingerprint,\n400\t    ) -> Vec<usize> {\n401\t        let mut divergent = Vec::new();\n402\t\n403\t        if fp_a.bucket_hashes.len() != BUCKET_COUNT || fp_b.bucket_hashes.len() != BUCKET_COUNT {\n404\t            // Fallback: if bucket hashes aren't computed, treat all buckets as divergent\n405\t            warn!(\n406\t                \"Bucket hashes not computed, treating all {} buckets as divergent\",\n407\t                BUCKET_COUNT\n408\t            );\n409\t            return (0..BUCKET_COUNT).collect();\n410\t        }\n411\t\n412\t        for (bucket_id, (hash_a, hash_b)) in fp_a\n413\t            .bucket_hashes\n414\t            .iter()\n415\t            .zip(fp_b.bucket_hashes.iter())\n416\t            .enumerate()\n417\t        {\n418\t            if hash_a != hash_b {\n419\t                divergent.push(bucket_id);\n420\t            }\n421\t        }\n422\t\n423\t        divergent\n424\t    }\n425\t\n426\t    /// Fetch all primary keys in a specific bucket (plan §13.8 step 2).\n427\t    ///\n428\t    /// Returns a map of primary key to content hash for all documents\n429\t    /// in the given bucket on the specified replica.\n430\t    pub async fn fetch_bucket_pks(\n431\t        &self,\n432\t        node_id: &NodeId,\n433\t        shard_id: u32,\n434\t        bucket_id: usize,\n435\t        index_uid: &str,\n436\t        address: &str,\n437\t    ) -> Result<HashMap<String, u64>> {\n438\t        let batch_size = self.config.fingerprint_batch_size as usize;\n439\t        let mut offset = 0u32;\n440\t        let mut bucket_pks = HashMap::new();\n441\t\n442\t        loop {\n443\t            let filter = serde_json::json!({ \"_miroir_shard\": shard_id });\n444\t            let request = FetchDocumentsRequest {\n445\t                index_uid: index_uid.to_string(),\n446\t                filter,\n447\t                limit: batch_size as u32,\n448\t                offset,\n449\t            };\n450\t\n451\t            let response: FetchDocumentsResponse = self\n452\t                .node_client\n453\t                .fetch_documents(node_id, address, &request)\n454\t                .await\n455\t                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Topology(format!(\"fetch failed: {:?}\", e)))?;\n456\t\n457\t            if response.results.is_empty() {\n458\t                break;\n459\t            }\n460\t\n461\t            for doc in &response.results {\n462\t                // Skip expired documents (plan §13.14 interaction)\n463\t                if self.config.ttl_enabled && Self::is_document_expired(doc) {\n464\t                    continue;\n465\t                }\n466\t\n467\t                let primary_key = doc\n468\t                    .get(\"id\")\n469\t                    .or(doc.get(\"_id\"))\n470\t                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n471\t                    .unwrap_or(\"\");\n472\t\n473\t                // Check if this document belongs to the target bucket\n474\t                let doc_bucket = Self::bucket_for_primary_key(primary_key);\n475\t                if doc_bucket == bucket_id {\n476\t                    let content_hash = Self::compute_content_hash(doc);\n477\t                    bucket_pks.insert(primary_key.to_string(), content_hash);\n478\t                }\n479\t            }\n480\t\n481\t            offset += batch_size as u32;\n482\t            tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_millis(10)).await;\n483\t        }\n484\t\n485\t        Ok(bucket_pks)\n486\t    }\n487\t\n488\t    /// Compare replicas within a divergent bucket to find specific PK differences.\n489\t    ///\n490\t    /// Returns a ReplicaDiff listing:\n491\t    /// - PKs only in replica A\n492\t    /// - PKs only in replica B\n493\t    /// - PKs with different content hashes\n494\t    pub async fn compare_bucket_replicas(\n495\t        &self,\n496\t        shard_id: u32,\n497\t        bucket_id: usize,\n498\t        node_a: &NodeId,\n499\t        address_a: &str,\n500\t        node_b: &NodeId,\n501\t        address_b: &str,\n502\t        index_uid: &str,\n503\t    ) -> Result<ReplicaDiff> {\n504\t        let pks_a = self\n505\t            .fetch_bucket_pks(node_a, shard_id, bucket_id, index_uid, address_a)\n506\t            .await?;\n507\t        let pks_b = self\n508\t            .fetch_bucket_pks(node_b, shard_id, bucket_id, index_uid, address_b)\n509\t            .await?;\n510\t\n511\t        let mut a_only = Vec::new();\n512\t        let mut b_only = Vec::new();\n513\t        let mut mismatched = Vec::new();\n514\t\n515\t        // Find PKs only in A or with mismatched content\n516\t        for (pk, hash_a) in &pks_a {\n517\t            match pks_b.get(pk) {\n518\t                Some(hash_b) if hash_b != hash_a => {\n519\t                    mismatched.push(pk.clone());\n520\t                }\n521\t                None => {\n522\t                    a_only.push(pk.clone());\n523\t                }\n524\t                _ => {}\n525\t            }\n526\t        }\n527\t\n528\t        // Find PKs only in B\n529\t        for pk in pks_b.keys() {\n530\t            if !pks_a.contains_key(pk) {\n531\t                b_only.push(pk.clone());\n532\t            }\n533\t        }\n534\t\n535\t        Ok(ReplicaDiff {\n536\t            shard_id,\n537\t            a_only_pks: a_only,\n538\t            b_only_pks: b_only,\n539\t            mismatched_pks: mismatched,\n540\t        })\n541\t    }\n542\t\n543\t    /// Run a single reconciliation pass.\n544\t    pub async fn run_pass(&self) -> Result<ReconcilerPass> {\n545\t        let mut pass = ReconcilerPass {\n546\t            started_at: millis_now(),\n547\t            completed_at: 0,\n548\t            shards_scanned: 0,\n549\t            shards_with_drift: 0,\n550\t            repairs_performed: 0,\n551\t            errors: Vec::new(),\n552\t        };\n553\t\n554\t        // Set as current pass\n555\t        {\n556\t            let mut current = self.current_pass.write().await;\n557\t            *current = Some(pass.clone());\n558\t        }\n559\t\n560\t        let topology = self.topology.read().await;\n561\t        let shard_count = topology.shards;\n562\t        let replica_groups = topology.groups().count() as u32;\n563\t\n564\t        // Determine which shards to scan\n565\t        let all_shards: Vec<u32> = (0..shard_count).collect();\n566\t        let shards_to_scan = if let (Some(replica_group_id), Some(num_pods)) =\n567\t            (self.replica_group_id, self.num_pods)\n568\t        {\n569\t            // Mode A scaling: filter to rendezvous-owned shards\n570\t            all_shards\n571\t                .into_iter()\n572\t                .filter(|shard_id| {\n573\t                    // Shard belongs to this pod if shard_id % num_pods == replica_group_id\n574\t                    (*shard_id % num_pods) == replica_group_id\n575\t                })\n576\t                .collect()\n577\t        } else if self.config.shards_per_pass == 0 {\n578\t            // Scan all shards\n579\t            all_shards\n580\t        } else {\n581\t            // Scan a subset (for throttling)\n582\t            all_shards\n583\t                .into_iter()\n584\t                .take(self.config.shards_per_pass as usize)\n585\t                .collect()\n586\t        };\n587\t\n588\t        info!(\n589\t            \"Anti-entropy pass starting: {} shards to scan\",\n590\t            shards_to_scan.len()\n591\t        );\n592\t\n593\t        // Track total mismatches found\n594\t        let mut total_mismatches = 0u32;\n595\t\n596\t        // Scan each shard\n597\t        for shard_id in shards_to_scan {\n598\t            match self.scan_shard(&topology, shard_id).await {\n599\t                Ok((drift_detected, repairs, mismatches)) => {\n600\t                    pass.shards_scanned += 1;\n601\t                    if drift_detected {\n602\t                        pass.shards_with_drift += 1;\n603\t                    }\n604\t                    pass.repairs_performed += repairs;\n605\t                    total_mismatches += mismatches;\n606\t                }\n607\t                Err(e) => {\n608\t                    pass.errors.push(format!(\"shard {}: {}\", shard_id, e));\n609\t                }\n610\t            }\n611\t\n612\t            // Self-throttle: sleep between shards to target <2% CPU\n613\t            tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_millis(100)).await;\n614\t        }\n615\t\n616\t        pass.completed_at = millis_now();\n617\t\n618\t        // Emit metrics if callback is configured\n619\t        if let Some(ref callback) = self.metrics_callback {\n620\t            callback(\n621\t                \"miroir_antientropy_shards_scanned_total\",\n622\t                pass.shards_scanned as u64,\n623\t            );\n624\t            callback(\n625\t                \"miroir_antientropy_mismatches_found_total\",\n626\t                total_mismatches as u64,\n627\t            );\n628\t            callback(\n629\t                \"miroir_antientropy_docs_repaired_total\",\n630\t                pass.repairs_performed as u64,\n631\t            );\n632\t            callback(\n633\t                \"miroir_antientropy_last_scan_completed_seconds\",\n634\t                pass.completed_at / 1000,\n635\t            );\n636\t        }\n637\t\n638\t        // Archive pass\n639\t        {\n640\t            let mut history = self.pass_history.write().await;\n641\t            history.push(pass.clone());\n642\t            // Keep last 100 passes\n643\t            if history.len() > 100 {\n644\t                history.remove(0);\n645\t            }\n646\t        }\n647\t\n648\t        // Clear current pass\n649\t        {\n650\t            let mut current = self.current_pass.write().await;\n651\t            *current = None;\n652\t        }\n653\t\n654\t        info!(\n655\t            \"Anti-entropy pass completed: {} shards scanned, {} with drift, {} repairs\",\n656\t            pass.shards_scanned, pass.shards_with_drift, pass.repairs_performed\n657\t        );\n658\t\n659\t        Ok(pass)\n660\t    }\n661\t\n662\t    /// Scan a single shard for drift.\n663\t    ///\n664\t    /// Returns (drift_detected, repairs_performed, mismatches_found).\n665\t    async fn scan_shard(&self, topology: &Topology, shard_id: u32) -> Result<(bool, u32, u32)> {\n666\t        // For each replica group, get the assigned nodes\n667\t        let mut fingerprints = Vec::new();\n668\t\n669\t        for group in topology.groups() {\n670\t            let assigned = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf());\n671\t            for node_id in assigned {\n672\t                // Look up node address from topology\n673\t                let topology_guard = self.topology.read().await;\n674\t                let node = topology_guard\n675\t                    .node(&node_id)\n676\t                    .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::Topology(format!(\"node {} not found\", node_id)))?;\n677\t\n678\t                if !node.is_healthy() {\n679\t                    warn!(\"Node {} is not healthy, skipping fingerprint\", node_id);\n680\t                    continue;\n681\t                }\n682\t\n683\t                let address = node.address.clone();\n684\t                drop(topology_guard);\n685\t\n686\t                match self\n687\t                    .fingerprint_shard(&node_id, shard_id, &self.config.index_uid, &address)\n688\t                    .await\n689\t                {\n690\t                    Ok(fp) => fingerprints.push((node_id.clone(), address, fp)),\n691\t                    Err(e) => {\n692\t                        warn!(\n693\t                            \"Failed to fingerprint shard {} on node {}: {}\",\n694\t                            shard_id, node_id, e\n695\t                        );\n696\t                        // Continue with other nodes\n697\t                    }\n698\t                }\n699\t            }\n700\t        }\n701\t\n702\t        if fingerprints.is_empty() {\n703\t            // No readable replicas\n704\t            return Ok((false, 0, 0));\n705\t        }\n706\t\n707\t        // Compare fingerprints\n708\t        let (ref_node_id, ref_address, reference) = &fingerprints[0];\n709\t        let mut drift_detected = false;\n710\t        let mut repairs_performed = 0u32;\n711\t        let mut mismatches_found = 0u32;\n712\t\n713\t        for (node_id, address, fp) in &fingerprints[1..] {\n714\t            if fp.merkle_root != reference.merkle_root {\n715\t                drift_detected = true;\n716\t                debug!(\n717\t                    \"Shard {} drift detected: node {} has different merkle root\",\n718\t                    shard_id, node_id\n719\t                );\n720\t\n721\t                if self.config.auto_repair {\n722\t                    // Perform detailed diff and repair\n723\t                    match self\n724\t                        .repair_shard(\n725\t                            shard_id,\n726\t                            ref_node_id,\n727\t                            ref_address,\n728\t                            reference,\n729\t                            node_id,\n730\t                            address,\n731\t                            fp,\n732\t                        )\n733\t                        .await\n734\t                    {\n735\t                        Ok((repairs, mismatches)) => {\n736\t                            repairs_performed += repairs;\n737\t                            mismatches_found += mismatches;\n738\t                        }\n739\t                        Err(e) => {\n740\t                            error!(\n741\t                                \"Failed to repair shard {} on node {}: {}\",\n742\t                                shard_id, node_id, e\n743\t                            );\n744\t                        }\n745\t                    }\n746\t                }\n747\t            }\n748\t        }\n749\t\n750\t        Ok((drift_detected, repairs_performed, mismatches_found))\n751\t    }\n752\t\n753\t    /// Repair a shard by comparing replicas and applying fixes (plan §13.8 step 3).\n754\t    ///\n755\t    /// Returns (repairs_performed, mismatches_found).\n756\t    async fn repair_shard(\n757\t        &self,\n758\t        shard_id: u32,\n759\t        reference_node: &NodeId,\n760\t        reference_address: &str,\n761\t        reference_fp: &ShardFingerprint,\n762\t        target_node: &NodeId,\n763\t        target_address: &str,\n764\t        target_fp: &ShardFingerprint,\n765\t    ) -> Result<(u32, u32)> {\n766\t        debug!(\n767\t            \"Repairing shard {} on node {} (reference: node {})\",\n768\t            shard_id, target_node, reference_node\n769\t        );\n770\t\n771\t        // Step 1: Find divergent buckets using per-bucket hashes (plan §13.8 step 2)\n772\t        let divergent_buckets = self.diff_fingerprints(reference_fp, target_fp);\n773\t\n774\t        if divergent_buckets.is_empty() {\n775\t            // No bucket-level divergence (shouldn't happen if merkle roots differ)\n776\t            warn!(\n777\t                \"Shard {} merkle roots differ but no bucket divergence found\",\n778\t                shard_id\n779\t            );\n780\t            return Ok((0, 0));\n781\t        }\n782\t\n783\t        debug!(\n784\t            \"Shard {} has {} divergent buckets out of {}\",\n785\t            shard_id,\n786\t            divergent_buckets.len(),\n787\t            BUCKET_COUNT\n788\t        );\n789\t\n790\t        let mut total_repairs = 0u32;\n791\t        let mut total_mismatches = 0u32;\n792\t\n793\t        // Step 2: For each divergent bucket, enumerate divergent PKs\n794\t        for bucket_id in divergent_buckets {\n795\t            match self\n796\t                .compare_bucket_replicas(\n797\t                    shard_id,\n798\t                    bucket_id,\n799\t                    reference_node,\n800\t                    reference_address,\n801\t                    target_node,\n802\t                    target_address,\n803\t                    &self.config.index_uid,\n804\t                )\n805\t                .await\n806\t            {\n807\t                Ok(diff) => {\n808\t                    let total_divergent =\n809\t                        diff.a_only_pks.len() + diff.b_only_pks.len() + diff.mismatched_pks.len();\n810\t                    total_mismatches += total_divergent as u32;\n811\t\n812\t                    if total_divergent > 0 {\n813\t                        debug!(\n814\t                            \"Bucket {} in shard {}: {} divergent PKs ({} A-only, {} B-only, {} mismatched)\",\n815\t                            bucket_id,\n816\t                            shard_id,\n817\t                            total_divergent,\n818\t                            diff.a_only_pks.len(),\n819\t                            diff.b_only_pks.len(),\n820\t                            diff.mismatched_pks.len()\n821\t                        );\n822\t\n823\t                        // Step 3: For each divergent PK, apply repair (plan §13.8 step 3)\n824\t                        // Collect all divergent PKs\n825\t                        let mut all_divergent_pks = Vec::new();\n826\t                        all_divergent_pks.extend(diff.a_only_pks.iter().cloned());\n827\t                        all_divergent_pks.extend(diff.b_only_pks.iter().cloned());\n828\t                        all_divergent_pks.extend(diff.mismatched_pks.iter().cloned());\n829\t\n830\t                        // Repair each divergent PK\n831\t                        for pk in all_divergent_pks {\n832\t                            match self\n833\t                                .repair_divergent_pk(\n834\t                                    shard_id,\n835\t                                    &pk,\n836\t                                    reference_node,\n837\t                                    reference_address,\n838\t                                    target_node,\n839\t                                    target_address,\n840\t                                )\n841\t                                .await\n842\t                            {\n843\t                                Ok(repairs) => {\n844\t                                    total_repairs += repairs;\n845\t                                }\n846\t                                Err(e) => {\n847\t                                    error!(\n848\t                                        \"Failed to repair PK {} in shard {}: {}\",\n849\t                                        pk, shard_id, e\n850\t                                    );\n851\t                                }\n852\t                            }\n853\t                        }\n854\t                    }\n855\t                }\n856\t                Err(e) => {\n857\t                    warn!(\n858\t                        \"Failed to compare bucket {} in shard {}: {}\",\n859\t                        bucket_id, shard_id, e\n860\t                    );\n861\t                }\n862\t            }\n863\t        }\n864\t\n865\t        Ok((total_repairs, total_mismatches))\n866\t    }\n867\t\n868\t    /// Repair a single divergent primary key (plan §13.8 step 3).\n869\t    ///\n870\t    /// # Repair Logic (plan §13.8 step 3)\n871\t    ///\n872\t    /// For each divergent PK:\n873\t    /// 1. Read document from each replica\n874\t    /// 2. Check for TTL expiration:\n875\t    ///    - If ANY replica has `_miroir_expires_at <= now`: DELETE from all replicas\n876\t    ///    - This prevents zombie resurrection (plan §13.14 interaction)\n877\t    /// 3. Otherwise, pick authoritative version:\n878\t    ///    - Highest `_miroir_updated_at` wins\n879\t    ///    - Tiebreak: newest node task_uid\n880\t    /// 4. Write authoritative version to all replicas that disagree\n881\t    /// 5. Tag with `_miroir_origin: antientropy` for CDC suppression\n882\t    ///\n883\t    /// Returns the number of repairs performed (0-2).\n884\t    async fn repair_divergent_pk(\n885\t        &self,\n886\t        shard_id: u32,\n887\t        primary_key: &str,\n888\t        reference_node: &NodeId,\n889\t        reference_address: &str,\n890\t        target_node: &NodeId,\n891\t        target_address: &str,\n892\t    ) -> Result<u32> {\n893\t        debug!(\n894\t            \"Repairing PK {} in shard {} (reference: {}, target: {})\",\n895\t            primary_key, shard_id, reference_node, target_node\n896\t        );\n897\t\n898\t        // Step 1: Fetch document from both replicas\n899\t        let ref_doc = self\n900\t            .fetch_document_by_pk(reference_node, reference_address, shard_id, primary_key)\n901\t            .await?;\n902\t\n903\t        let target_doc = self\n904\t            .fetch_document_by_pk(target_node, target_address, shard_id, primary_key)\n905\t            .await?;\n906\t\n907\t        // Step 2: Check for TTL expiration (plan §13.14 interaction)\n908\t        // If ANY replica has an expired document, DELETE from all replicas\n909\t        let now_ms = millis_now();\n910\t        let ref_expired = ref_doc\n911\t            .as_ref()\n912\t            .and_then(|d| d.get(\"_miroir_expires_at\"))\n913\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n914\t            .map_or(false, |expires| expires <= now_ms);\n915\t\n916\t        let target_expired = target_doc\n917\t            .as_ref()\n918\t            .and_then(|d| d.get(\"_miroir_expires_at\"))\n919\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n920\t            .map_or(false, |expires| expires <= now_ms);\n921\t\n922\t        if ref_expired || target_expired {\n923\t            info!(\n924\t                \"PK {} has expired document (ref_expired={}, target_expired={}), deleting from all replicas\",\n925\t                primary_key, ref_expired, target_expired\n926\t            );\n927\t\n928\t            // Delete from all replicas\n929\t            let mut deletions = 0u32;\n930\t            if ref_doc.is_some() {\n931\t                self.delete_document_by_pk(reference_node, reference_address, primary_key)\n932\t                    .await?;\n933\t                deletions += 1;\n934\t            }\n935\t            if target_doc.is_some() {\n936\t                self.delete_document_by_pk(target_node, target_address, primary_key)\n937\t                    .await?;\n938\t                deletions += 1;\n939\t            }\n940\t\n941\t            return Ok(deletions);\n942\t        }\n943\t\n944\t        // Step 3: Pick authoritative version (highest _miroir_updated_at)\n945\t        let (authoritative_doc, authoritative_node) = match (&ref_doc, &target_doc) {\n946\t            (None, None) => {\n947\t                warn!(\"PK {} not found on either replica, nothing to repair\", primary_key);\n948\t                return Ok(0);\n949\t            }\n950\t            (Some(doc), None) => {\n951\t                debug!(\"PK {} only exists on reference node\", primary_key);\n952\t                (doc.clone(), reference_node.clone())\n953\t            }\n954\t            (None, Some(doc)) => {\n955\t                debug!(\"PK {} only exists on target node\", primary_key);\n956\t                (doc.clone(), target_node.clone())\n957\t            }\n958\t            (Some(ref_doc_val), Some(target_doc_val)) => {\n959\t                // Both exist - compare _miroir_updated_at\n960\t                let ref_updated = ref_doc_val\n961\t                    .get(&self.config.updated_at_field)\n962\t                    .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n963\t                    .unwrap_or(0);\n964\t\n965\t                let target_updated = target_doc_val\n966\t                    .get(&self.config.updated_at_field)\n967\t                    .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n968\t                    .unwrap_or(0);\n969\t\n970\t                if ref_updated >= target_updated {\n971\t                    debug!(\n972\t                        \"PK {} reference node wins (updated_at: {} vs {})\",\n973\t                        primary_key, ref_updated, target_updated\n974\t                    );\n975\t                    (ref_doc_val.clone(), reference_node.clone())\n976\t                } else {\n977\t                    debug!(\n978\t                        \"PK {} target node wins (updated_at: {} vs {})\",\n979\t                        primary_key, target_updated, ref_updated\n980\t                    );\n981\t                    (target_doc_val.clone(), target_node.clone())\n982\t                }\n983\t            }\n984\t        };\n985\t\n986\t        // Step 4: Write authoritative version to replicas that disagree\n987\t        let mut repairs_performed = 0u32;\n988\t\n989\t        // Check if reference needs repair\n990\t        if ref_doc.as_ref() != Some(&authoritative_doc) {\n991\t            if let Err(e) = self\n992\t                .write_document_with_origin(\n993\t                    reference_node,\n994\t                    reference_address,\n995\t                    &authoritative_doc,\n996\t                )\n997\t                .await\n998\t            {\n999\t                error!(\"Failed to write repair to reference node {}: {}\", reference_node, e);\n1000\t            } else {\n1001\t                repairs_performed += 1;\n1002\t                debug!(\"Repaired PK {} on reference node {}\", primary_key, reference_node);\n1003\t            }\n1004\t        }\n1005\t\n1006\t        // Check if target needs repair\n1007\t        if target_doc.as_ref() != Some(&authoritative_doc) {\n1008\t            if let Err(e) = self\n1009\t                .write_document_with_origin(target_node, target_address, &authoritative_doc)\n1010\t                .await\n1011\t            {\n1012\t                error!(\"Failed to write repair to target node {}: {}\", target_node, e);\n1013\t            } else {\n1014\t                repairs_performed += 1;\n1015\t                debug!(\"Repaired PK {} on target node {}\", primary_key, target_node);\n1016\t            }\n1017\t        }\n1018\t\n1019\t        if repairs_performed > 0 {\n1020\t            info!(\n1021\t                \"Repaired PK {} in shard {} on {} replica(s)\",\n1022\t                primary_key, shard_id, repairs_performed\n1023\t            );\n1024\t        }\n1025\t\n1026\t        Ok(repairs_performed)\n1027\t    }\n1028\t\n1029\t    /// Pick the authoritative document between two versions (plan §13.8 step 3).\n1030\t    ///\n1031\t    /// # Selection Logic\n1032\t    ///\n1033\t    /// 1. Highest `_miroir_updated_at` wins\n1034\t    /// 2. Tiebreak: newest node task_uid (if available)\n1035\t    /// 3. Final tiebreak: deterministic content hash comparison\n1036\t    ///\n1037\t    /// Returns `Some(&authoritative_doc)` if one or both docs exist, `None` if both are None.\n1038\t    pub fn pick_authoritative_doc<'a>(\n1039\t        &'a self,\n1040\t        doc_a: &'a Value,\n1041\t        doc_b: &'a Value,\n1042\t    ) -> Option<&'a Value> {\n1043\t        match (doc_a.is_null(), doc_b.is_null()) {\n1044\t            (true, true) => None,\n1045\t            (true, false) => Some(doc_b),\n1046\t            (false, true) => Some(doc_a),\n1047\t            (false, false) => {\n1048\t                // Both exist - compare _miroir_updated_at\n1049\t                let updated_a = doc_a\n1050\t                    .get(&self.config.updated_at_field)\n1051\t                    .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n1052\t                    .unwrap_or(0);\n1053\t\n1054\t                let updated_b = doc_b\n1055\t                    .get(&self.config.updated_at_field)\n1056\t                    .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n1057\t                    .unwrap_or(0);\n1058\t\n1059\t                if updated_a > updated_b {\n1060\t                    Some(doc_a)\n1061\t                } else if updated_b > updated_a {\n1062\t                    Some(doc_b)\n1063\t                } else {\n1064\t                    // Tie - use content hash for deterministic selection\n1065\t                    let hash_a = Self::compute_content_hash(doc_a);\n1066\t                    let hash_b = Self::compute_content_hash(doc_b);\n1067\t                    if hash_a >= hash_b {\n1068\t                        Some(doc_a)\n1069\t                    } else {\n1070\t                        Some(doc_b)\n1071\t                    }\n1072\t                }\n1073\t            }\n1074\t        }\n1075\t    }\n1076\t\n1077\t    /// Fetch a single document by primary key from a node.\n1078\t    async fn fetch_document_by_pk(\n1079\t        &self,\n1080\t        node_id: &NodeId,\n1081\t        address: &str,\n1082\t        shard_id: u32,\n1083\t        primary_key: &str,\n1084\t    ) -> Result<Option<Value>> {\n1085\t        // Build filter to find the document by PK and shard\n1086\t        let filter = serde_json::json!({\n1087\t            \"_miroir_shard\": shard_id,\n1088\t            \"id\": primary_key,\n1089\t        });\n1090\t\n1091\t        let request = FetchDocumentsRequest {\n1092\t            index_uid: self.config.index_uid.clone(),\n1093\t            filter,\n1094\t            limit: 1,\n1095\t            offset: 0,\n1096\t        };\n1097\t\n1098\t        match self\n1099\t            .node_client\n1100\t            .fetch_documents(node_id, address, &request)\n1101\t            .await\n1102\t        {\n1103\t            Ok(response) => {\n1104\t                if let Some(doc) = response.results.first() {\n1105\t                    Ok(Some(doc.clone()))\n1106\t                } else {\n1107\t                    Ok(None)\n1108\t                }\n1109\t            }\n1110\t            Err(e) => {\n1111\t                // Document not found is not an error for anti-entropy\n1112\t                debug!(\"PK {} not found on node {}: {:?}\", primary_key, node_id, e);\n1113\t                Ok(None)\n1114\t            }\n1115\t        }\n1116\t    }\n1117\t\n1118\t    /// Write a document with the anti-entropy origin tag for CDC suppression.\n1119\t    async fn write_document_with_origin(\n1120\t        &self,\n1121\t        node_id: &NodeId,\n1122\t        address: &str,\n1123\t        document: &Value,\n1124\t    ) -> Result<()> {\n1125\t        let request = WriteRequest {\n1126\t            index_uid: self.config.index_uid.clone(),\n1127\t            documents: vec![document.clone()],\n1128\t            primary_key: None,\n1129\t            origin: Some(ORIGIN_ANTIENTROPY.to_string()),\n1130\t        };\n1131\t\n1132\t        self.node_client\n1133\t            .write_documents(node_id, address, &request)\n1134\t            .await\n1135\t            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Topology(format!(\"write failed: {:?}\", e)))?;\n1136\t\n1137\t        Ok(())\n1138\t    }\n1139\t\n1140\t    /// Delete a document by primary key with the anti-entropy origin tag.\n1141\t    async fn delete_document_by_pk(\n1142\t        &self,\n1143\t        node_id: &NodeId,\n1144\t        address: &str,\n1145\t        primary_key: &str,\n1146\t    ) -> Result<()> {\n1147\t        let request = crate::scatter::DeleteByIdsRequest {\n1148\t            index_uid: self.config.index_uid.clone(),\n1149\t            ids: vec![primary_key.to_string()],\n1150\t            origin: Some(ORIGIN_ANTIENTROPY.to_string()),\n1151\t        };\n1152\t\n1153\t        self.node_client\n1154\t            .delete_documents(node_id, address, &request)\n1155\t            .await\n1156\t            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Topology(format!(\"delete failed: {:?}\", e)))?;\n1157\t\n1158\t        debug!(\"Deleted PK {} from node {}\", primary_key, node_id);\n1159\t        Ok(())\n1160\t    }\n1161\t\n1162\t    /// Cross-index bucket comparison for reshard verification (plan §13.1 step 4).\n1163\t    ///\n1164\t    /// Compares two indexes with potentially different shard counts (e.g., live vs shadow\n1165\t    /// during resharding). Uses PK-keyed bucketing (pk-hash % 256) which is independent\n1166\t    /// of the shard count, enabling cross-S comparison.\n1167\t    ///\n1168\t    /// Returns a ReplicaDiff listing divergent PKs across all buckets.\n1169\t    /// This reuses §13.8's bucketed-Merkle machinery but operates across indexes\n1170\t    /// rather than within a single shard.\n1171\t    pub async fn compare_index_buckets(\n1172\t        &self,\n1173\t        node_a: &NodeId,\n1174\t        address_a: &str,\n1175\t        index_a: &str,\n1176\t        shard_count_a: u32,\n1177\t        node_b: &NodeId,\n1178\t        address_b: &str,\n1179\t        index_b: &str,\n1180\t        shard_count_b: u32,\n1181\t    ) -> Result<ReplicaDiff> {\n1182\t        let mut all_a_only = Vec::new();\n1183\t        let mut all_b_only = Vec::new();\n1184\t        let mut all_mismatched = Vec::new();\n1185\t\n1186\t        // Fetch all PKs and their content hashes from both indexes, bucketed by PK\n1187\t        let bucket_pks_a = self.fetch_all_index_pks_bucketed(node_a, address_a, index_a, shard_count_a).await?;\n1188\t        let bucket_pks_b = self.fetch_all_index_pks_bucketed(node_b, address_b, index_b, shard_count_b).await?;\n1189\t\n1190\t        // Compare each bucket\n1191\t        for bucket_id in 0..BUCKET_COUNT {\n1192\t            let pks_a = &bucket_pks_a[bucket_id];\n1193\t            let pks_b = &bucket_pks_b[bucket_id];\n1194\t\n1195\t            // Find PKs only in A or with mismatched content\n1196\t            for (pk, hash_a) in pks_a {\n1197\t                match pks_b.get(pk) {\n1198\t                    Some(hash_b) if hash_b != hash_a => {\n1199\t                        all_mismatched.push(pk.clone());\n1200\t                    }\n1201\t                    None => {\n1202\t                        all_a_only.push(pk.clone());\n1203\t                    }\n1204\t                    _ => {}\n1205\t                }\n1206\t            }\n1207\t\n1208\t            // Find PKs only in B\n1209\t            for pk in pks_b.keys() {\n1210\t                if !pks_a.contains_key(pk) {\n1211\t                    all_b_only.push(pk.clone());\n1212\t                }\n1213\t            }\n1214\t        }\n1215\t\n1216\t        Ok(ReplicaDiff {\n1217\t            shard_id: 0, // Not applicable for cross-index comparison\n1218\t            a_only_pks: all_a_only,\n1219\t            b_only_pks: all_b_only,\n1220\t            mismatched_pks: all_mismatched,\n1221\t        })\n1222\t    }\n1223\t\n1224\t    /// Fetch all primary keys from an index, organized by PK-keyed bucket.\n1225\t    ///\n1226\t    /// This function scans all shards of the index and organizes documents\n1227\t    /// by their PK-hash bucket (0..255), independent of shard assignment.\n1228\t    /// Used for cross-index comparison during reshard verification.\n1229\t    async fn fetch_all_index_pks_bucketed(\n1230\t        &self,\n1231\t        node_id: &NodeId,\n1232\t        address: &str,\n1233\t        index_uid: &str,\n1234\t        shard_count: u32,\n1235\t    ) -> Result<Vec<HashMap<String, u64>>> {\n1236\t        let batch_size = self.config.fingerprint_batch_size as usize;\n1237\t        let mut bucket_pks: Vec<HashMap<String, u64>> = (0..BUCKET_COUNT)\n1238\t            .map(|_| HashMap::new())\n1239\t            .collect();\n1240\t\n1241\t        // Iterate through all shards in the index\n1242\t        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n1243\t            let mut offset = 0u32;\n1244\t\n1245\t            loop {\n1246\t                let filter = serde_json::json!({ \"_miroir_shard\": shard_id });\n1247\t                let request = FetchDocumentsRequest {\n1248\t                    index_uid: index_uid.to_string(),\n1249\t                    filter,\n1250\t                    limit: batch_size as u32,\n1251\t                    offset,\n1252\t                };\n1253\t\n1254\t                let response: FetchDocumentsResponse = self\n1255\t                    .node_client\n1256\t                    .fetch_documents(node_id, address, &request)\n1257\t                    .await\n1258\t                    .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Topology(format!(\"fetch failed: {:?}\", e)))?;\n1259\t\n1260\t                if response.results.is_empty() {\n1261\t                    break;\n1262\t                }\n1263\t\n1264\t                for doc in &response.results {\n1265\t                    let primary_key = doc\n1266\t                        .get(\"id\")\n1267\t                        .or(doc.get(\"_id\"))\n1268\t                        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n1269\t                        .unwrap_or(\"\");\n1270\t\n1271\t                    if primary_key.is_empty() {\n1272\t                        continue;\n1273\t                    }\n1274\t\n1275\t                    let content_hash = Self::compute_content_hash(doc);\n1276\t                    let bucket_id = Self::bucket_for_primary_key(primary_key);\n1277\t\n1278\t                    bucket_pks[bucket_id].insert(primary_key.to_string(), content_hash);\n1279\t                }\n1280\t\n1281\t                offset += batch_size as u32;\n1282\t                tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_millis(10)).await;\n1283\t            }\n1284\t        }\n1285\t\n1286\t        Ok(bucket_pks)\n1287\t    }\n1288\t\n1289\t    /// Get pass history.\n1290\t    pub async fn pass_history(&self) -> Vec<ReconcilerPass> {\n1291\t        self.pass_history.read().await.clone()\n1292\t    }\n1293\t\n1294\t    /// Get current pass in progress.\n1295\t    pub async fn current_pass(&self) -> Option<ReconcilerPass> {\n1296\t        self.current_pass.read().await.clone()\n1297\t    }\n1298\t}\n1299\t\n1300\t/// Validates that migration is safe given the anti-entropy configuration.\n1301\t/// Returns Ok(()) if safe, Err with a descriptive message if not.\n1302\t///\n1303\t/// Hard refusal policy (plan §15 OP#1): skipping the delta pass while\n1304\t/// anti-entropy is disabled provides zero recovery path for documents\n1305\t/// written at the cutover boundary. Measured loss rate: ~2% of writes.\n1306\t/// This is a hard-coded policy, not a warning.\n1307\tpub fn validate_migration_safety(\n1308\t    ae_config: &AntiEntropyConfig,\n1309\t    migration_config: &MigrationConfig,\n1310\t) -> std::result::Result<(), MigrationError> {\n1311\t    if migration_config.skip_delta_pass && !ae_config.enabled {\n1312\t        return Err(MigrationError::UnsafeCutoverNoAntiEntropy);\n1313\t    }\n1314\t    Ok(())\n1315\t}\n1316\t\n1317\t/// Generates a warning if anti-entropy is disabled during active migration.\n1318\t/// The caller should log this at warn level.\n1319\t///\n1320\t/// Even with the delta pass enabled (which provides 0-loss cutover on its own),\n1321\t/// disabling anti-entropy means the delta pass is the sole safety mechanism.\n1322\t/// Operators should be aware of this reduced redundancy.\n1323\tpub fn migration_warning_if_ae_disabled(ae_enabled: bool) -> Option<String> {\n1324\t    if ae_enabled {\n1325\t        return None;\n1326\t    }\n1327\t    Some(\n1328\t        \"Anti-entropy is disabled. Shard migration cutover relies on the delta pass \\\n1329\t         as the sole safety mechanism. Any bugs in the delta pass could lead to \\\n1330\t         data loss at the cutover boundary. Re-enable anti-entropy for defense-in-depth.\"\n1331\t            .to_string(),\n1332\t    )\n1333\t}\n1334\t\n1335\t/// Get current UNIX timestamp in milliseconds.\n1336\tfn millis_now() -> u64 {\n1337\t    SystemTime::now()\n1338\t        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n1339\t        .unwrap_or_default()\n1340\t        .as_millis() as u64\n1341\t}\n1342\t\n1343\t#[cfg(test)]\n1344\tmod tests {\n1345\t    use super::*;\n1346\t    use serde_json::json;\n1347\t\n1348\t    #[test]\n1349\t    fn test_validate_safe_with_delta_pass() {\n1350\t        let ae = AntiEntropyConfig {\n1351\t            enabled: false,\n1352\t            ..Default::default()\n1353\t        };\n1354\t        let mc = MigrationConfig {\n1355\t            skip_delta_pass: false,\n1356\t            ..Default::default()\n1357\t        };\n1358\t        assert!(validate_migration_safety(&ae, &mc).is_ok());\n1359\t    }\n1360\t\n1361\t    #[test]\n1362\t    fn let mc = MigrationConfig {\n1368\t            skip_delta_pass: true,\n1369\t            anti_entropy_enabled: false,\n1370\t            ..Default::default()\n1371\t        };\n1372\t        assert!(validate_migration_safety(&ae, &mc).is_err());\n1373\t    }\n1374\t\n1375\t    #[test]\n1376\t    fn test_validate_safe_with_anti_entropy_safety_net() {\n1377\t        let ae = assert!(migration_warning_if_ae_disabled(true).is_none());\n1393\t    }\n1394\t\n1395\t    #[test]\n1396\t    fn test_compute_content_hash_strips_internal_fields() {\n1397\t        let
